name: PERCEIVE
description: PERCEIVE (Psychophysics-grounded Elicitation for Routing Cost-Efficiency In Vision-Language Evaluation) is a VLM routing benchmark of 4,801 document-image QA samples across 16 public datasets and 6 task families. Each sample is annotated with psychophysical complexity scores (Visual Dependency Score, Reasoning Depth Score, Spatial Extent Score on an ordinal 1-4 scale), stratified into Easy, Medium, and Hard tiers via Bayesian threshold estimation, and evaluated across 7 commercial VLMs at 4 reasoning-budget levels (28 configurations). Routing labels indicating the cheapest-correct model-budget configuration per query are derived via a QUEST-style adaptive cascade achieving 60.7% cost reduction with 100% ground-truth label agreement. The benchmark includes a 1,500-sample exhaustively-evaluated anchor set (split=anchor), a 750-sample held-out validation set (split=validation), and 2,551 test queries (split=test). Supports incremental model-pool extension via Inductive Matrix Completion.
cr:dataCollection: Samples are derived from 16 publicly available document-image datasets spanning six task families. No new images were collected; all source images were obtained through their respective official distribution channels (HuggingFace Hub or original dataset repositories). A stratified submodular facility-location sampler selected 4,801 samples from a candidate pool of 15,482, targeting a 25/50/25 Easy/Medium/Hard tier distribution. Psychophysical complexity annotations (VDS, RDS, SES) were elicited automatically from a three-model probe oracle (GPT-4o, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, Gemini-1.5-Pro) using a structured prompt (Appendix C of the paper). No human annotators were employed for complexity scoring.
cr:dataCollectionType: Derived dataset: samples selected and re-annotated from pre-existing public datasets. No new data collection from human subjects.
cr:dataAnnotationProtocol: Ground-truth routing labels were derived via a QUEST-style adaptive cascade (Appendix D). Three probe models independently estimate VDS/RDS/SES scores; a Bayesian posterior over psychophysical thresholds assigns each sample to a difficulty tier. Model-budget evaluations use a symbolic-neural arbiter: rule-based scorers (ANLS, exact match, IoU, F1) with a Mistral-based neural fallback for ambiguous cases and a GPT-4o oracle arbiter for conflicts. Routing labels were cross-validated against the 750-sample validation set, achieving 100% ground-truth agreement on the DVR metric.
cr:dataAnnotationPlatform: Fully automated pipeline; no crowdsourcing platforms used. All annotation was performed programmatically using commercial VLM APIs (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, Mistral) under their respective terms of service.
cr:machineAnnotationTools: Probe oracle: GPT-4o, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, Gemini-1.5-Pro. Neural judge: Mistral-Large. Oracle arbiter: GPT-4o. Routing evaluation models: Claude-3.5-Haiku, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, Claude-3.7-Sonnet, Gemini-2.0-Flash, Gemini-2.0-Pro, GPT-4o-mini, GPT-4o (each at 4 reasoning-budget levels).
cr:annotatorDemographics: No human annotators. Complexity scores are machine-elicited from three commercial VLMs. Source dataset ground-truth answers are inherited from original dataset releases created by their respective research teams; demographic information about those original annotators is not available in PERCEIVE.
cr:dataUseCases: Intended for: (1) benchmarking VLM routing and cost-efficiency strategies on document-understanding tasks; (2) evaluating psychophysics-grounded complexity metrics for vision-language tasks; (3) studying the relationship between task complexity and model capability across document types; (4) training and validating routing classifiers. Not intended for: training generative models, evaluating general-purpose VLM capability (no open-ended generation tasks), or human subject research.
cr:dataLimitations: Source coverage: 16 document-image datasets spanning commercial documents, scientific publications, and web images; natural scene images and medical imaging are not represented. Language: English only. Model pool: 7 commercial VLMs as of early 2026; IMC generalises to new models within known document types (AUC 0.876) but degrades on novel document types (AUC 0.60 holdout). Unroutable ceiling: 20.7% of samples are unanswerable by any configuration in the pool. Probe stability: tier assignments are stable (>90%) under single-model dropout and 10% perturbation.
cr:personalSensitiveInformation: No personally identifiable information. Source datasets contain document images from public domain sources. PERCEIVE adds no PII beyond what is present in the original datasets.
cr:ethicsReviewProcess: Dataset derived entirely from publicly available datasets under their respective licenses. No human subjects research conducted. No new web scraping. Commercial API usage performed under vendor terms of service. Dataset contains no hate speech, adult content, or content targeting protected groups.
cr:dataBiases: Source datasets skew toward English-language Western business documents (invoices, receipts, forms) and academic publications. Chart and table datasets reflect US/European data conventions. These biases are inherited from source datasets and documented here to guide appropriate use.

dct:source.name: DocVQA
dct:source.dct:rights: DocVQA license — academic research use only.

dct:source.name: InfographicVQA
dct:source.dct:rights: InfographicVQA license — academic research use only.

dct:source.name: RVL-CDIP
dct:source.dct:rights: CC BY-SA 3.0 Non-Commercial. PERCEIVE releases only derived metadata; source images must be obtained separately.

dct:source.name: MP-DocVQA
dct:source.dct:rights: MP-DocVQA license — academic research use only.

dct:source.name: VisualMRC
dct:source.dct:rights: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. PERCEIVE releases only derived metadata; source images must be obtained separately.

dct:source.name: HierText
dct:source.dct:rights: CC BY-SA 4.0. PERCEIVE releases only derived metadata annotations.

dct:source.name: WikiTableQuestions
dct:source.dct:rights: CC BY-SA 4.0. Wikipedia-derived; PERCEIVE releases only derived annotations.

dct:source.name: TabFact
dct:source.dct:rights: CC BY-SA 4.0. Wikipedia-derived; PERCEIVE releases only derived annotations.

distribution.name: benchmark_5000.jsonl
distribution.description: 4,801 document-image QA samples with VDS/RDS/SES complexity annotations, difficulty tier (1-3), visual-element flags, split membership (anchor/validation/test), and evaluation metadata.

distribution.name: routing_labels.jsonl
distribution.description: 4,801 routing labels: cheapest-correct model-budget configuration per sample, derived via QUEST-style adaptive cascade. Includes is_routable flag and eval_signal.

distribution.name: final_eval_correct.jsonl
distribution.description: 77,719 per-(sample, model, budget) correctness judgments covering the anchor and validation sets across all 28 configurations plus external validation models.

distribution.name: oracle_verdicts.jsonl
distribution.description: 7,974 oracle arbiter decisions for ambiguous evaluation cases where rule-based scorers and neural judge disagreed.

distribution.name: all_models_judgments_v2.jsonl
distribution.description: 78,308 raw neural judge outputs for (sample, model, budget) pairs including rule-based scorer decisions and Mistral-Large neural fallback responses.

distribution.name: probe_results.jsonl
distribution.description: Probe oracle elicitation outputs: VDS, RDS, SES scores from each of the three probe models (GPT-4o, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, Gemini-1.5-Pro) for all 4,801 samples.

recordSet.name: benchmark_records
recordSet.description: Records from benchmark_5000.jsonl. Each record is one document-image QA sample with psychophysical complexity annotations and split assignment.

field.name: sample_id
field.description: Unique identifier combining source dataset name and index.

field.name: split
field.description: Dataset split: anchor (1,500 exhaustively-evaluated samples), validation (750 held-out), or test (2,551 cascade-labeled queries).

field.name: source_dataset
field.description: Source dataset name. One of: DocVQA, SlideVQA, TextVQA, ChartQA, SROIE, InfographicVQA, PubLayNet, RVL-CDIP, MP-DocVQA, ST-VQA, VisualMRC, FUNSD, HierText, WTQ, CORD, TabFact.

field.name: task_type
field.description: Task family: T1 (document classification), T2 (structured field extraction), T3 (layout and spatial reasoning), T4 (semantic and compositional QA), T5 (multi-page QA), T6 (element localization).

field.name: query
field.description: Natural-language question about the document image.

field.name: gt_answer
field.description: Primary ground-truth answer, inherited from source dataset.

field.name: correctness_metric
field.description: Evaluation metric: anls, exact_match, field_f1, or iou.

field.name: tier_final
field.description: Difficulty tier: 1 (Easy), 2 (Medium), 3 (Hard). Assigned by Bayesian posterior over VDS/RDS/SES probe scores.

field.name: vds_probe_avg
field.description: Visual Dependency Score (1-4): averaged across three probe models. Measures reliance on visual features beyond OCR text.

field.name: rds_probe_avg
field.description: Reasoning Depth Score (1-4): averaged across three probe models. Measures reasoning steps required.

field.name: ses_probe_avg
field.description: Spatial Extent Score (1-4): averaged across three probe models. Measures fraction of document that must be attended.

field.name: has_table
field.description: Boolean: document image contains a structured table.

field.name: has_chart
field.description: Boolean: document image contains a chart or graph.

field.name: image_path
field.description: Relative path to document image within the source dataset archive.

recordSet.name: routing_label_records
recordSet.description: Records from routing_labels.jsonl. Each record identifies the cheapest-correct model-budget configuration per sample.

field.name: cheapest_correct_model
field.description: YAML key of cheapest model answering correctly. Null if unroutable.

field.name: cheapest_correct_budget
field.description: Budget tier of cheapest correct config: B0 (0 tokens), B1 (1,024), B2 (4,096), B3 (16,384). Null if unroutable.

field.name: is_routable
field.description: Boolean: at least one model-budget configuration answers correctly. False for the 20.7% unroutable ceiling.

field.name: split
field.description: Split membership: anchor, validation, or test.
